content.text:
Good. Now I have a clear picture. Here are the 4 info pop-ups (English):

| Panel | Key | Text |
|-------|-----|------|
| Kitchen Appliances | `kitchenAppliancesInfo` | "Add special appliances like an air fryer or Vitamix. The AI will use these to tailor cooking methods. Saved on this device for future sessions." |
| Pantry | `pantryInfo` | "Add your main ingredients here. The planner will try to use all of these ingredients in the generated recipes." |
| Spice Rack | `spiceRackInfo` | "Add staples like spices, oils, or salt. These items will not necessarily be used in the recipes, won't be added to your shopping list and are saved on this device for future sessions." |
| Shopping List (decoupled) | `decoupledInfo` | "This shopping list is different from your most recently generated one. Checkmarks are stored separately and won't sync with your main list." |

The inconsistencies I see:

1. **Structure**: Some mention persistence ("Saved on this device"), some don't. Pantry doesn't mention it even though items are also persisted.
2. **Tone**: Varies between instructional ("Add...") and descriptive ("This shopping list is..."). The decoupled info is a status explanation, not an instructional tooltip — it's a different kind of info, so that one can reasonably differ.
3. **Length**: Spice rack and kitchen appliances pack 3 concepts into one sentence. Pantry is short.
4. **Pattern**: The three input panels (pantry, spice rack, appliances) should follow the same structure since they serve similar purposes.

**Proposed consistent style for the 3 input panels:**

Each tooltip should cover: (1) what to add, (2) how the AI uses it. Persistence info is redundant since everything persists — drop it.

- **Kitchen Appliances**: "Add appliances like an air fryer or Vitamix. The AI will tailor cooking methods to what you have available."
- **Pantry**: "Add your main ingredients here. The AI will try to use all of them in the generated recipes."
- **Spice Rack**: "Add staples like spices, oils, or salt. The AI may use these but won't add them to your shopping list."

The **decoupled info** is different in nature (a contextual notice, not a usage hint), so I'd leave its structure as-is but tighten it: "This shopping list differs from your latest generated one. Checkmarks are stored separately."

What do you think of this direction? Any preferences before I apply it across all 4 languages?

message.content: I agree with dropping the persistent message from the three settings infos. Your proposed kitchen appliances text is not entirely correct: the AI knows about these and might use them, but it isn't specifically guided by them. The spices, in turn, do guide the AI. Can you check the prompt (in services/llm.tsx) as to what the AI exactly does with each input?

content.text:
Now I can see exactly how each input is used in the prompt:

- **Pantry items**: "Prioritize using as many of my pantry ingredients as possible." (Rule 2). They're the core ingredients the AI tries to use up.
- **Spices/Staples**: "Let the available spices and staples **guide** the recipes. Not all spices or staples need to be used." (Rule 9). They're marked "Do NOT add to shopping list." They actively shape recipe direction.
- **Appliances**: Simply listed as "Available Special Kitchen Appliances" — no specific rule about them. The AI is aware of them but there's no explicit instruction to use or be guided by them.

So the revised texts:

- **Pantry**: "Add your main ingredients here. The AI will try to use all of them in the generated recipes."
- **Spice Rack**: "Add staples like spices, oils, or salt. These guide the recipes but won't be added to your shopping list."
- **Kitchen Appliances**: "Add appliances like an air fryer or Vitamix. The AI is aware of them and may suggest recipes that use them."

Does that match your understanding?

message.content: Much more, yes. Can you propose German translations?

content.text:
- **Pantry**: "Füge hier deine Hauptzutaten hinzu. Die KI wird versuchen, alle davon in den Rezepten zu verwenden."
- **Spice Rack**: "Füge Grundvorräte wie Gewürze, Öle oder Salz hinzu. Diese leiten die Rezepte, werden aber nicht auf die Einkaufsliste gesetzt."
- **Kitchen Appliances**: "Füge Geräte wie eine Heissluftfritteuse oder einen Vitamix hinzu. Die KI kennt sie und kann Rezepte vorschlagen, die sie nutzen."

message.content: I would like to rephrase the Pantry text again: these are not "main" ingredients, are they? They are the "available ingredients", or is there a better term?

content.text:
You're right. Looking at the prompt, they're simply "ingredients in my pantry" — everything you have on hand. "Available ingredients" works, or even just "your ingredients." 

How about:

- **EN**: "Add your available ingredients here. The AI will try to use all of them in the generated recipes."
- **DE**: "Füge hier deine verfügbaren Zutaten hinzu. Die KI wird versuchen, alle davon in den Rezepten zu verwenden."

message.content: Yes, that's good. Propose the French translation, please.

content.text:
- **Pantry**: "Ajoutez vos ingrédients disponibles ici. L'IA essaiera de tous les utiliser dans les recettes générées."
- **Spice Rack**: "Ajoutez des produits de base comme des épices, des huiles ou du sel. Ceux-ci orientent les recettes, mais ne seront pas ajoutés à votre liste de courses."
- **Kitchen Appliances**: "Ajoutez des appareils comme une friteuse à air ou un Vitamix. L'IA en tient compte et peut suggérer des recettes qui les utilisent."

- **Pantry**: "Agrega tus ingredientes disponibles aquí. La IA intentará usar todos en las recetas generadas."
- **Spice Rack**: "Agrega productos básicos como especias, aceites o sal. Estos guían las recetas, pero no se agregarán a tu lista de compras."
- **Kitchen Appliances**: "Agrega electrodomésticos como una freidora de aire o Vitamix. La IA los tiene en cuenta y puede sugerir recetas que los utilicen."

message.content: Sounds good. Go ahead and implement this.

message.content: You also mentioned the shopping list text. Let's look at this again.

content.text:
The current `decoupledInfo` texts:

| Lang | Text |
|------|------|
| EN | "This shopping list is different from your most recently generated one. Checkmarks are stored separately and won't sync with your main list." |
| DE | "Diese Einkaufsliste unterscheidet sich von deiner zuletzt generierten. Häkchen werden separat gespeichert und synchronisieren nicht mit deiner Hauptliste." |
| FR | "Cette liste de courses est différente de celle générée le plus récemment. Les cases cochées sont stockées séparément et ne se synchroniseront pas avec votre liste principale." |
| ES | "Esta lista de compras es diferente de la generada más recientemente. Las marcas de verificación se almacenan por separado y no se sincronizarán con tu lista principal." |

This one is different from the other three — it's a contextual notice rather than a usage hint. The core message is: "this is a shared list with its own independent checkmarks."

I'd tighten it to:

- **EN**: "This is a shared shopping list. Checkmarks are stored separately from your main list."
- **DE**: "Dies ist eine geteilte Einkaufsliste. Häkchen werden separat von deiner Hauptliste gespeichert."
- **FR**: "Ceci est une liste de courses partagée. Les cases cochées sont stockées séparément de votre liste principale."
- **ES**: "Esta es una lista de compras compartida. Las marcas se almacenan por separado de tu lista principal."

Shorter, and leads with *what it is* rather than *how it differs*. What do you think?

message.content: I don't think that users would understand the difference between "shared" and "main" list, would they?

content.text:
You're right. From the user's perspective, they opened a shopping list someone shared with them — they don't know there's a "main" list concept. The key thing they need to understand is simply that their checkmarks are local to them and won't affect anyone else's.

How about:

- **EN**: "This shopping list was shared with you. Your checkmarks are saved only on this device."
- **DE**: "Diese Einkaufsliste wurde mit dir geteilt. Deine Häkchen werden nur auf diesem Gerät gespeichert."
- **FR**: "Cette liste de courses a été partagée avec vous. Vos cases cochées sont enregistrées uniquement sur cet appareil."
- **ES**: "Esta lista de compras fue compartida contigo. Tus marcas se guardan solo en este dispositivo."

message.content: This is much better, yes. Can we still add something that the checkmarks are seperate from "your own list", but keeping the tight style?

content.text:
- **EN**: "This shopping list was shared with you. Your checkmarks are saved only on this device and won't affect your own list."
- **DE**: "Diese Einkaufsliste wurde mit dir geteilt. Deine Häkchen werden nur auf diesem Gerät gespeichert und beeinflussen deine eigene Liste nicht."
- **FR**: "Cette liste de courses a été partagée avec vous. Vos cases cochées sont enregistrées uniquement sur cet appareil et n'affectent pas votre propre liste."
- **ES**: "Esta lista de compras fue compartida contigo. Tus marcas se guardan solo en este dispositivo y no afectan tu propia lista."

tooltips for consistency. Each tooltip now accurately reflects how the
AI uses the input based on the actual prompt logic. Removed redundant
persistence mentions. Updated all 4 languages (EN, DE, FR, ES).

EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Standardize info tooltip texts across all panels" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Rewrote pantry, spice rack, kitchen appliances, and shopping list info tooltips for consistency across all 4 languages (EN, DE, FR, ES)
- Each tooltip now accurately reflects how the AI uses the input, based on the actual prompt logic in `buildRecipePrompt()`
- Removed redundant persistence/device storage mentions from the three input panel tooltips

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify each info tooltip displays correctly in all 4 languages
- [ ] Confirm shared shopping list shows the updated decoupled info text

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
